commentary: Twelve activities this week versus nine last week, and that is not a coincidence — that is a man who has decided something. The long run on Saturday was 13.5km against last week's 13.1km, which is a quiet little flex, and the swim heart rates are genuinely coming down: Monday's 2000m sat at 132 and Tuesday's at 144, compared to 145 and 148 for the same sessions last week, which means the pool is starting to feel like somewhere you belong rather than somewhere you're surviving. The "Recovery" run with a 150 average heart rate is being called out by name because that set of quotation marks is doing a lot of heavy lifting and everyone can see them. More bodies in more pools, more bars, more weight rooms — the pattern here is someone who is simply showing up more, and that is the whole thing, that is literally all it ever is.
metrics_commentary: Twenty-point-seven kilometres across two sessions and over seven and a half thousand metres in the pool — honestly, the audacity of him, doing all that while presumably still looking incredible. The HRV sitting at 51 with a seven-day average of 52 is solid, it says his body is recovering well and not quietly screaming at him, which is the kind of boring good news that actually matters. The VO2 Max at 57.3 is genuinely impressive, that is a number that belongs to someone whose cardiovascular system has opinions about itself, strong ones. The only thing worth watching is whether two run sessions is enough frequency to keep building that base, because the swim is clearly doing the heavy lifting and legs deserve to be in on the fun too.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, four done — Scott's target hit clean, no excuses, no gaps, just a man who showed up every single time this week. Seven personal records in one week is not a normal thing that happens, and yet here we are: leg press up to 168 kg, bicep curl cracking 35 kg, seated cable row at 84 kg, both dumbbell bench and incline bench breaking through, Bulgarian split squats and hammer curls joining the party — that is a body that is changing, and it knows it. Weekly volume came in at 4,045 kg which is a significant drop from the 24,518 kg the week prior, so the volume dipped hard, and that's worth watching — but seven PRs in a lower-volume week suggests the quality of effort was absolutely there even if the quantity wasn't. The becoming is very much on schedule, honey.
